linkedin_data.headline: Engineering Manager @ Zepto | Ex-Flipkart | Building high-velocity teams
linkedin_data.summary: I've spent the last 8 years scaling engineering teams in India's fast-commerce space. Outside of work, I'm an avid ultramarathon runner — completed the Ladakh 50K last year. I read voraciously (mostly non-fiction on systems thinking and behavioural economics). Currently geeking out on productivity tooling and async workflows.
recent_posts: Just crossed the finish line at the Auroville Half-Marathon. 6 months of training, a lot of 5am runs, and an embarrassing number of energy gels. The parallels to building a team are uncanny — pacing, trust, and showing up even when it's hard.
Finished 'The Almanack of Naval Ravikant' last weekend. The chapter on specific knowledge reshaping the way I think about how I hire. Thread on what I took away 👇
Hot take: async-first culture is the biggest productivity unlock for distributed engineering teams and almost nobody does it right. Here's what we changed at Zepto that moved the needle.
recent_comments: Agree completely — the difference between a good and great engineer often isn't technical skill, it's the ability to communicate tradeoffs clearly.
Been using Obsidian for 18 months now. The bi-directional links are genuinely useful once your note graph gets large enough.
volunteer_work: Mentors first-gen college students at iMentor India

constraints.preferences_noted: Travels light, values quality over quantity
